i'm not sure i actually agree this is true - she responded extremely negatively to the keeper's assertion that she was trying to ply other beings with (assumed aphrodisiac) drinks, asking "do you really see me as so despicable?"

it makes her a more interesting character, in my opinion. a lot of dialogues indicate thais as a character with a very "different" understanding to most humans of sex, reproduction, pleasure... also flesh-weaving and devouring. despite being her domain, she thinks of it in terms of and asserts its necessity first before she considers more pleasurable aspects.

it's also what makes her intimidating, since she truly feels morally justified in her... behavior towards the keeper at certain points. it is both her 'right' due to the life they previously shared, and a 'necessity', or so she sees.

of course i am not saying she doesn't find other characters cute or attractive, especially conceptually, but in terms of actively pursuing them for her own wants? that's something much different. i would describe this as a contrast to for example wanda and sorel, both of whom are very confident in their sexuality and would clearly pursue any that they wished to.
